paragraphs.text:
“Perfect typography is certainly the most elusive of all arts. Sculpture in stone alone comes near it in obstinacy.”
        — Jan Tschichold, 1948

visibleText: Forma Negra . Review Cover Feature Critique Typographica Archive Vol. 12 / Friction Typographica · Anatomy Plate Nº 03 3 Seven landmarks of a single glyph. 01 Cap height — uppercase ceiling, 700 units 02 x-height — lowercase mean, 510 units 03 Descender — depth below baseline, 200 u 04 Serif bracket — transitional curvature 05 Counter — enclosed negative space 06 Stem — primary vertical stroke 07 Ink trap — concavity for press gain Glossary · 18 terms Aperture Opening of a partially enclosed counter. Apex Top junction of two strokes, as in /A. Ascender Stroke rising above x-height. Bowl Curved stroke enclosing a counter. Bracket Curve joining serif to stem. Counter Negative space within a glyph. Crossbar Horizontal stroke spanning two stems. Descender Stroke dropping below baseline. Ear Small projection from the bowl of /g. Finial Tapered terminal of a stroke. Ink trap Concavity compensating for spread. Kerning Pair-specific letterfit adjustment. Ligature Glyph joining two characters. Spur Small projection at stroke base. Stem Primary vertical or diagonal stroke. Stress Axis of contrast in curved strokes. Terminal Stroke end without a serif. x-height Height of lowercase /x.
